title: Capital Flight Map: Where the Money Goes
summary: Comprehensive mapping of Argentine capital flight channels, destinations, and volumes. Argentina's residents hold an estimated USD 483 billion in external assets (INDEC Q3 2025), making the country a net international creditor despite chronic fiscal deficits. This paradox is the structural fingerprint of decades of capital flight.

indec_external_assets_q3_2025.source: INDEC - Posicion de Inversion Internacional, Q3 2025

informal_dollar_holdings_estimate.note: Includes cash outside the financial system ('colchon'), unregistered foreign deposits, safety deposit boxes

banking_fae.label: Formacion de Activos Externos (BCRA balance cambiario)
banking_fae.description: Official BCRA measure of resident purchases of foreign assets through the formal exchange market. The primary legal channel for capital outflows.

2024_full_year.note: Positive record during cepo cambiario restrictions

2025_jan_jul_post_unification.note: Highest since MULC creation in 2002. Accelerated after April 2025 exchange rate unification.

vs_imf_disbursement.note: In 4 months post-unification, FAE exceeded total IMF disbursements

persons_cash_purchases_jul_2025.note: Individual dollar purchases alone in one month

real_estate_miami.label: Argentine Real Estate Investment in Miami/South Florida
real_estate_miami.description: Argentines are the #1 or #2 foreign buyer group in South Florida, purchasing primarily with cash (68%), indicating wealth already held offshore.

annual_investment_volume_2024.note: Total Argentine investment in Florida in 2024

data.growth_2025: 200% increase in platform activity H1 2025 vs H2 2024

uruguay_deposits.label: Argentine Deposits and Real Estate in Uruguay
uruguay_deposits.description: Uruguay is the nearest offshore haven. Deposits fluctuate with Argentine political cycles. Punta del Este real estate is a major store of offshore wealth.

non_resident_deposits_peak.note: Total non-resident deposits (majority Argentine) - peak before 2023 drawdown

argentines_direct_deposits.note: Argentine nationals specifically, plus USD 58M in Patagonia Uruguay (IFE Punta del Este)

data.trend: Deposits fell H1 2023 as Argentines moved funds further from AFIP reach (US, Switzerland). Rebounding post-blanqueo.

swiss_banking.label: Swiss Banking (Credit Suisse / UBS)
swiss_banking.description: Switzerland remains a key destination. The Suisse Secrets leak revealed dozens of Argentine account holders. UBS settled USD 511M with DOJ in 2025 over Credit Suisse's offshore tax evasion assistance.

data.suisse_secrets_argentines: Several dozen Argentine beneficiaries among 18,000+ accounts linked to the leak

ubs_doj_settlement_2025.note: UBS guilty plea for Credit Suisse helping wealthy clients hide $4B+ offshore

data.blanqueo_2024_swiss_origin: Switzerland among top 4 countries of origin for declared blanqueo assets

delaware_shell_companies.label: Delaware LLCs as Opacity Vehicle
delaware_shell_companies.description: Delaware requires no beneficial ownership disclosure at state level. LLCs can be formed for $50 with a registered agent hiding the true owner. Used extensively by Latin American wealth holders.

data.beneficial_ownership_disclosure: None at state level. Federal CTA requirements in flux after litigation.
data.use_case: Real estate purchases, brokerage accounts, holding structures to anonymize Argentine-origin wealth in US markets

crypto_stablecoins.label: Cryptocurrency and Stablecoins as Capital Flight Channel
crypto_stablecoins.description: Argentina ranks #1 in Latin America for crypto volume and #20 globally. Stablecoins (USDT, USDC) account for 61.8% of all crypto activity, functioning as a parallel dollar market and capital flight vehicle.

total_crypto_volume_2024.note: Total crypto received by Argentine users Jul 2023-Jun 2024, per Chainalysis

data.p2p_dominance: P2P exchanges (Binance P2P, local platforms) are primary on/off ramp, allowing peso-to-USDT conversion outside formal banking
data.capital_flight_mechanism: Buy USDT on P2P with pesos -> transfer to self-custodial wallet or foreign exchange -> convert to USD/other. No BCRA reporting. No FAE registration.

mining_transfer_pricing.label: Mining Sector Transfer Pricing and Subfacturing
mining_transfer_pricing.description: Argentina's mining royalty cap of 3% on 'boca de mina' value, combined with allowable cost deductions, creates a structural incentive for transfer pricing abuse. Lithium subfacturing has been documented with export prices at 1/3 of market value.

data.deduction_loophole: Law 25.161 (1999) allows deduction of direct/operational costs from royalty base, substantially reducing effective royalty rate

soy_complex_transfer_pricing.note: Research on 2004-2020 period found largest deviations during rising international prices - exporters delayed price adjustments to extend subfacturing windows

glencore_rigi_applications.note: Glencore seeking RIGI tax incentives for $13B in projects - further reducing fiscal capture

tax_evasion_scale.iva_evasion_trend: Fell from 47%+ to below 40% for first time, driven by digitalization

ley_inocencia_fiscal.note: Thresholds raised 66x for simple evasion and 66x for aggravated. 4,199 pending cases dropped.

blanqueo_as_indicator.description: Tax amnesty programs reveal the scale of hidden wealth. The 2024 Milei blanqueo collected USD 32B in declarations - but this is a fraction of the estimated USD 250-480B held offshore or outside the system.
blanqueo_as_indicator.comparison_note: Macri 2016 declared 3.6x more but at higher tax rates. Milei 2024 had faster cash inflow velocity. Combined, both blanqueos declared USD 149B - still less than half the estimated offshore stock.

balance_of_payments_shift.description: The current account swung from USD 6.3B surplus in 2024 to deep deficit in 2025, driven by capital outflows post-unification and tourism spending.

channel_summary_table.description: Estimated annual flows by channel (order of magnitude)

channels.note: Total volume, not all is capital flight. Stablecoin portion (~61%) partially represents peso-to-dollar conversion outside banking
Stock, not flow. Fluctuates with political cycles.
Total mining sector losses likely multiples higher including copper, gold, soy
channels.visibility: Partially detectable via customs price analysis

channels.note: Blanqueo origin data suggests billions. Suisse Secrets revealed dozens of accounts.
Opacity vehicle. No state-level disclosure. Enables anonymous US real estate and brokerage.

key_findings: Argentina's residents hold USD 483B in external assets (INDEC Q3 2025) - roughly equal to the country's GDP. The country is a net international creditor by USD 50B despite having USD 44B in sovereign external debt.
In just 4 months after exchange rate unification (Apr-Jul 2025), FAE outflows of USD 14.7B exceeded total IMF disbursements of USD 12B - the IMF loan effectively financed capital flight.
The crypto channel moved USD 91B through Argentine users in one year, with 61.8% in stablecoins. P2P trading makes much of this invisible to BCRA and ARCA.
Two blanqueos (2016 + 2024) declared a combined USD 149B - but this is less than half the estimated USD 250-480B held outside the system, indicating the declared amount is a floor, not a ceiling.
The Ley de Inocencia Fiscal raised evasion prosecution thresholds by 66x and dropped 4,199 pending cases - effectively decriminalizing most tax evasion during a period of record capital outflows.

Lithium export subfacturing alone cost USD 403M in 2024. With mining royalties capped at 3% and RIGI incentives reducing it further, the fiscal capture on a USD 13B Glencore investment pipeline is structurally minimal.
The blanqueo origin countries (US, Uruguay, Spain, Switzerland) map precisely to the offshore destination network: Miami real estate, Montevideo/Punta del Este deposits, and Swiss banking.
